rationale: The schema is implementable as a conservative extension of 109's internal/config structs: Panel can gain Note/Gates, Reviewer can gain Model/Lens while preserving Family/Count, Substitution can gain Substitutes, and the three gate resolvers return creation-time defaults without importing config into internal/panel or reinterpreting thresholds. The R4 refusal matrix composes cleanly with 109 R5: global reviewers/threshold remain bounded by 109, while per-gate reviewer sums, gate-local thresholds, inherited global integer thresholds, closed gate keys, and substitute map shape add stricter checks only where 109 had no per-gate surface. No model/lens enum validation conflicts with 109's free-form model posture. The main implementation footgun is YAML presence tracking for optional count defaulting to 1 while explicit count <= 0 is refused; this likely needs custom unmarshalling or pointer/raw-node handling, but the spec and ACs make the intended behavior falsifiable.

findings.summary: Optional count defaulting requires YAML field-presence tracking.
findings.details: 109 stores Reviewer.Count as int and rejects zero. 112 allows omitted count as 1 but refuses explicit 0/negative. A bead implementer must not rely on the plain zero value after yaml.Unmarshal; use custom unmarshalling, a pointer in an intermediate struct, or yaml.Node inspection.

findings.summary: Resolver signatures are sound and keep threshold interpretation single-homed.
findings.details: PanelGateExpectedReviewers and PanelGateReviewerSlots compute sums/slots; PanelGateApproveThresholdExpr returns the raw expression only. This preserves 109's single interpreter in internal/panel.Panel.ApproveThreshold and avoids a config-side decision dependency.

findings.summary: R4 refusals do not conflict with 109 R5 refusals.
findings.details: 109 still rejects invalid global thresholds, invalid global reviewer counts, and global reviewer sums below 2. 112 adds per-gate equivalents plus inherited-integer threshold checks for gates with different reviewer sums, which closes the per-gate always-pass/unsatisfiable cases without weakening legacy configs.
